sourceMovie.title: The Lost Boys
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Lost Boys (1987) stuck because it commits to its horror premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Joel Schumacher's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The horror and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: It
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Lost Boys clicked for you, It (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Lost Boys?
faq.answer: Try It, Day Shift, Jamon Jamon—each shares DNA with The Lost Boys while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Lost Boys?
faq.answer: The Lost Boys (1987) is best described as Horror, Comedy, Thriller, Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Lost Boys part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Lost Boys Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
